query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "training long-context vision-language models effectively with generali" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (d) and (f) as a foundational review and canonical reference for the domain of Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs), which is the core subject of the user's question regarding "Long-Context Vision-Language Models." Although the paper focuses on object detection rather than the specific "long-context" training efficiency mechanism mentioned in the user's title, it provides the essential state-of-the-art baseline, architectural context, and training paradigm overview
